candidate_problems.text: Can constraint-coupling density be operationalized so that two explanations of the same phenomenon can be compared quantitatively for hard-to-vary structure?
Are there explanations with high dependency entanglement that are nevertheless wrong, and if so what additional property distinguishes merely intricate error from genuine hard-to-vary knowledge?
When does paraphrase preserve explanatory structure versus secretly altering a dependency that matters for downstream consequences?
Can an LLM-based search process be trained or scaffolded to optimize for constraint-saturated explanations rather than for coherence, elegance, or human approval?
